name: Avilés Estuary and Ferrera Park Loop
name_original: Ría de Avilés y Parque de Ferrera — circular desde la Plaza España de Avilés
description: Jog this moderate 3.9-mile circular route from Plaza España, exploring the green Parque de Ferrera and the scenic Ría de Avilés.
region_intro: Jogging around Avilés offers a diverse environment, blending urban green spaces with scenic natural features. The region is characterized by its estuary, which connects the city to the sea, and numerous public parks like Ferrera Park and Parque del Muelle. Coastal areas, including Salinas Beach, provide extensive running paths, while inland, the Trasona Reservoir offers circular routes around its waters. This variety ensures a range of terrains for runners, from paved paths to natural trails. Best jogging routes around Avilés The most popular jogging route is Avilés Estuary and Ferrera Park Loop, a 3.9 miles (6.3 km) trail that takes 39 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a picturesque backdrop along the estuary and through the historic Ferrera Park. Another top favourite among local runners is Niemeyer Center ️ Avilés loop from Cristalería, a moderate 6.1 miles (9.9 km) path. This route integrates the prominent architectural landmark of the Niemeyer Center into a scenic urban loop, often completed in about 1 hour 1 minute. Local runners also love the San Nicolás de Bari  Avilés – Plaza de España, Avilés loop from Avilés Apeadero, a 4.2 miles (6.7 km) trail leading through the city center and past historic landmarks, often completed in about 42 minutes. Jogging around Avilés is defined by its estuary paths, extensive urban parks, and coastal routes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy, paved surfaces to more challenging trails. The routes in Avilés are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 1000 runners have used komoot to explore Avilés's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Avilés?
A: Avilés offers a wide variety of options for runners, with over 30 distinct routes available. These routes cater to different preferences, ranging from moderate urban loops to more challenging trails.

Q: Are there any easy jogging routes suitable for beginners in Avilés?
A: While most routes are classified as moderate, many sections along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056057643/aviles-estuary-and-ferrera-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Avilés Estuary and Ferrera Park Loop&lt;/a&gt; offer comfortable, paved, and tamped surfaces ideal for beginners. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/427897" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque de Ferrera&lt;/a&gt; itself has a 925-meter circular cross-country circuit that is very accessible.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in Avilés?
A: Yes, Avilés has several excellent circular running routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056057643/aviles-estuary-and-ferrera-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Avilés Estuary and Ferrera Park Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.9 miles (6.3 km). For those seeking inland water views, the Pantano de Trasona offers a 4300-meter circular path.

Q: Are the jogging trails in Avilés family-friendly?
A: Many of the routes in Avilés are suitable for families, especially those through the city's numerous parks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/427897" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque de Ferrera&lt;/a&gt; and Parque del Muelle. These green spaces provide safe, pleasant environments with amenities, making them ideal for family outings.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the jogging trails in Avilés?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many of the outdoor trails and in parks in Avilés, provided they are kept on a leash and owners clean up after them. Specific regulations may apply in certain protected areas or during peak times, so it's always good to check local signage.

Q: What kind of landmarks or points of interest can I see while running in Avilés?
A: Avilés offers a rich blend of natural beauty and architectural landmarks. You can run past the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15000548" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oscar Niemeyer International Cultural Center&lt;/a&gt;, explore the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/427897" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ferrera Park&lt;/a&gt; with its sculptures and fountains, or enjoy coastal views near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5584287" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Peñona Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and the Museo de Anclas Philippe Cousteau.

Q: Are there routes that offer coastal views or beach access?
A: Absolutely! The Avilés Estuary connects the city to the sea, providing picturesque coastal backdrops. &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5039958" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa María del Mar Beach&lt;/a&gt; and Salinas Beach are excellent options, with Salinas Beach featuring a 6000-meter linear cross-country running route. The Senda Costera (Coastal Path) also offers extensive seaside running.

Q: Where can I find routes with good parking facilities?
A: Many of the urban parks and popular running areas in Avilés, such as those near the Avilés Estuary and the Oscar Niemeyer International Cultural Center, have accessible parking. For routes further afield like Pantano de Trasona, dedicated parking areas are usually available for visitors.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging routes in Avilés?
A: The running routes in Avilés are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 1000 runners have used komoot to explore Avilés's varied terrain, often praising the scenic estuary paths and the well-maintained urban parks.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by the Oscar Niemeyer International Cultural Center?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15000548" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Niemeyer Center 🖼️ Avilés loop from Cristalería&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate 6.1 miles (9.9 km) path that integrates this prominent architectural landmark into a scenic urban loop. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38209119" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Niemeyer Center 🖼️ Avilés loop from Llaranes&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Avilés?
A: Avilés generally enjoys a mild climate, making it suitable for jogging year-round. Spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant temperatures. Summer can be warm but coastal breezes often provide relief, while winters are typically mild, though rain is more common.

Q: Can I find routes that take me through the historic city center?
A: Certainly! The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15000416" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Nicolás de Bari ⛪ Avilés – Plaza de España, Avilés loop from Avilés Apeadero&lt;/a&gt; is a 4.2 miles (6.7 km) trail that leads directly through the city center, allowing you to jog past historic landmarks and experience the urban charm of Avilés.
tips: The historic center of Avilés for centuries, also known by the nickname "El Parche''. It is considered the zero kilometer of Avilés. The most typical and busiest streets of the city start from it: La Ferrería, La Cámara, Rivero, San Francisco and The Fruit.

The Avilés estuary is a natural estuary that flows into the sea through San Juan de Nieva. Along the Avilés estuary you can see different cultural elements, for example the San Sebastian Bridge, and a series of seven sculptures by local artists made from waste materials from the Arcelor factory (the steel route).

A public park located in the heart of the city. The park is named after Pedro Menéndez de Avilés, a famous Spanish explorer who founded the city of San Agustín in Florida. It was designed in the 19th century by the French landscape designer Eugène Belgrand, and was inaugurated in 1894. It is known for its beautiful gardens, large ponds, sculptures, fountains, and historical monuments. It also has a children's playground and hosts various cultural events throughout the year, including concerts, festivals and exhibitions.

faqs.question: Where can I park when starting this jogging route from Plaza España?
faqs.answer: The route begins at the historic Plaza de España, Avilés. While there isn't dedicated parking directly at the plaza, you can find public parking garages or street parking in the surrounding areas of Avilés city center. Consider using public transport to reach the starting point if you prefer not to search for parking.

faqs.question: Is this jogging route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is generally dog-friendly. Dogs are typically allowed on leash in public parks and along the Ría de Avilés promenade. Please ensure you clean up after your pet to maintain the cleanliness of the areas.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: This route is enjoyable year-round, but spring and autumn offer the most pleasant temperatures for jogging. Summers can be warm, while winters are generally mild. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Parque de Ferrera or the Ría de Avilés route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access either the Ferrera Park 🌳 Avilés or the Avilés Estuary promenade. Both are public spaces freely accessible to everyone.

faqs.question: What are the operating hours and amenities available in Parque de Ferrera?
faqs.answer: The Ferrera Park 🌳 Avilés is open daily from 7 AM to 11 PM. Within the park, you'll find an illuminated jogging circuit, exercise stations, public bathrooms (though they may be closed on holidays), and a drinking fountain.

faqs.question: Can I jog this route at night?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can jog parts of this route at night. The circular track within Ferrera Park 🌳 Avilés is illuminated, making it suitable for evening runs. The park itself is open until 11 PM.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this jogging route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features paved surfaces. The section through Ferrera Park 🌳 Avilés is a dedicated circuit with slight uphill and downhill sections, designed to be less impactful than asphalt. The Avilés Estuary promenade is mostly flat, consisting of concrete and asphalt, with a very gentle incline on the outbound path.

faqs.question: Is this jogging route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While Komoot categorizes this as a "moderate" run, its relatively low elevation gain and predominantly flat or gently undulating terrain make it manageable for most joggers, including beginners with a reasonable fitness level. The varied surfaces offer a good introduction to urban jogging.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll start at the historic Plaza de España, Avilés. The route then takes you through the beautiful Ferrera Park 🌳 Avilés, with its tranquil pond and ancient trees. Along the Avilés Estuary, you'll see metallic sculptures from the "Ruta del Acero" and the striking architecture of the Niemeyer Cultural Center.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Northern Route (Asturia), Mtb Northern Route (Asturia), and various segments of the Senda ciclista (cycling path).

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the starting point at Plaza España?
faqs.answer: Yes, Plaza de España, Avilés is in the heart of the city, so you'll find numerous cafes, bars, and restaurants in the immediate vicinity where you can grab a drink or a meal before or after your run.
